stories.headline: UAE leaves OPEC in blow to oil cartel amid war on Iran
stories.summary: Announcement comes as Gulf producers are already struggling to ship exports through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: UAE to leave Opec in blow to oil cartel

stories.headline: UAE to Leave OPEC in May as Iran War Reshapes Oil Market
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates will leave OPEC, dealing a blow to the group and its leader Saudi Arabia as the global oil industry grapples with the massive supply disruption caused by the Iran war.

stories.headline: Ukraine-Israel row deepens over Russian shipments of stolen grain
stories.summary: In a continuation of the ongoing diplomatic uproar, Zelenskyy publicly criticised Israel on Tuesday for considering a purchase of Ukrainian grain stolen by Russia from temporarily occupied territories in southern Ukraine.

stories.headline: ‘War crime’: Afghan-Pakistan truce under strain after university strike
stories.summary: Civilian casualties in Kunar spur tensions as Pakistan denies role, casting shadow over ceasefire and peace talks.

stories.headline: Ukraine accuses Israel of receiving shipments of grain 'stolen' by Russia
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says it has received no evidence to support the claim that a vessel carrying grain from occupied Ukraine has reached Haifa.

stories.headline: One year after Spain’s blackout, its shift to renewables and grid evolution power on
stories.summary: Though solar was initially incorrectly blamed for crisis, renewables have helped insulate Spain from gas price rises caused by war in Middle EastOne year ago today, all of Spain, and much of Portugal, suffered through a blackout of unprecedented scale and duration. stories.headline: Belarus eyes Western ties as it frees journalist Andrzej Poczobut

stories.headline: Amnesty calls for US strike on Yemen to be investigated as war crime

Iran war will trigger largest energy price surge since 2022, World Bank warns

stories.headline: UAE leaves OPEC, citing national interest in 'a new energy age'
stories.summary: The UAE’s historic decision is a major blow to the alliance of oil producers, just as the global energy crisis is escalating over Iran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, but the Gulf state insists its decision “enhances the UAE’s ability to respond to evolving market needs."

stories.headline: EU 'ready' to sanction Israel over Russian vessel carrying stolen Ukrainian grain
stories.summary: A diplomatic crisis has erupted between Ukraine and Israel following the arrival in Haifa of a Russian vessel reportedly carrying stolen Ukrainian grain. Brussels says it is ready to sanction those involved.

stories.headline: NZ axes plan for WW2 sex slaves statue after Japan protest
stories.summary: Tokyo had warned that installing the structure in Auckland could jeopardise diplomatic relations.

stories.headline: Sherwin-Williams Sees Input Costs Rising After Sales Beat
stories.summary: Sherwin-Williams Co. warned rising input costs could weigh on results later this year, after currency tailwinds helped it post stronger-than-expected sales growth in the first quarter.

stories.headline: Barclays Eyes Over 50% Recovery on MFS as Asset Hunt Uncertain
stories.summary: Barclays Plc hopes to get back much of the £500 million ($674 million) it lent to Market Financial Solutions Ltd. even as insolvency officials overseeing the now-collapsed mortgage lender remain uncertain on how much can be recovered.

stories.summary: The state visit of King Charles III comes at a moment of tension over the war in Iran between President Trump and Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

stories.headline: Energy price surge slams countries in debt to IMF

stories.headline: Japan Airlines trials humanoid robots as ground handlers
stories.summary: These robots may in future help clean cabins and operate ground support equipment.

stories.headline: Citadel Books More US Rockies Gas Transport on Key Pipeline
stories.summary: Citadel Energy Marketing, the merchant trading arm of Ken Griffin’s Citadel, has expanded its natural gas pipeline transportation capacity on a key interstate conduit from the Rocky Mountains to Arizona by 37%, positioning itself ahead of anticipated growth in Southwest gas demand.

stories.headline: Netherlands Rejects Argentina Soy Meal Cargoes on GMO Issue
stories.summary: The Netherlands has rejected at least two Argentinian soybean meal cargoes this month after non-approved genetically modified material was found, threatening to disrupt a major trade flow.

stories.headline: Airtel Eyes $2 Billion London IPO of Mobile Money Unit
stories.summary: Airtel Africa Plc’s initial public offering of its mobile money business could raise between $1.5 billion and $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Bertelsmann to Buy Concord, Creating $14 Billion Music Giant
stories.summary: German media giant Bertelsmann SE agreed to buy Nashville-based independent music group Concord and merge it with its BMG Rights Management business in a deal that will create one of the largest music companies in the world.

stories.headline: Cosan’s Compass Seeks to End 5-Year Brazil IPO Drought
stories.summary: Compass Gas e Energia SA is seeking to raise as much as 3.1 billion reais ($622 million) in what would be the first initial public offering in Brazilian stock markets in almost five years.

stories.summary: The elections are still six months off, and some within the G.O.P. say there is still time to right the ship.

stories.headline: European Parliament calls to define rape as sex without consent
stories.summary: A resolution adopted by the European Parliament calls for a common definition of rape across the EU, stating that any sexual act without consent should be considered rape.

stories.headline: Euronews explains: What are eurobonds, why is it divisive and does it make sense?
stories.summary: What are eurobonds, and how could they help the European economy? Why is France pushing for more joint debt, and will it work? Euronews examines the case behind an asset that central banks and economists increasingly say makes economic sense but remains politically toxic for the EU.

stories.headline: Deutsche Telekom eyes full T-Mobile merger in what could be the biggest deal in history
stories.summary: Deutsche Telekom already owns 53% of T-Mobile US. Now it wants the rest — in what could become the biggest corporate merger in history.

stories.headline: Oil Back Above $110 as World Awaits US Response to Iran Proposal
stories.summary: Oil rose to the highest level since just before a ceasefire deal between the US and Iran, as traders await Washington’s response to a proposal from Tehran to end the war potentially reopen of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: US strike kills three on alleged narco boat as campaign death toll hits 185
stories.summary: Military video shows boat moving swiftly in water before explosion leaves it in flamesThe US military said on Sunday three men were killed when it struck a boat it claimed was “engaged in narco-trafficking operations” in the Eastern Pacific Ocean.This latest strike – which follows dozens of similar attacks on alleged drug boats in recent months – brings the US campaign’s death toll to at least 185, according to a tally compiled by Agence France-P

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: Investigators in Trump Assassination Attempt Look Through Bluesky for Suspect’s Motive
stories.summary: Federal authorities are looking into whether Cole Tomas Allen posted on Bluesky as “coldforce,” who wrote and promoted liberal views that did not stand out on the left-leaning platform.

stories.headline: Republicans Push for Trump’s White House Ballroom After Gala Attack
stories.summary: The attack on a press dinner in Washington, which is being called an attempted assassination of President Trump, has also renewed the fight over reopening the Homeland Security Department.

stories.headline: Canada's Carney launches a sovereign wealth fund. What is it?
stories.summary: The fund - which Canadians can invest in directly - will help pay for major infrastructure projects in the country, the prime minister says.

stories.headline: Stocks Fall as Report on OpenAI Fuels Tech Jitters: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A selloff in technology giants dragged down stocks as concerns resurfaced over whether vast investments in artificial intelligence will pay off just as traders gear up for a slew of megacap results.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there

stories.headline: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

stories.headline: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested

stories.headline: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
